mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in labor demand signals an expansionary fiscal policy cycle that compresses interest rates and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in labor supply signals a contractionary monetary policy cycle that compresses discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals an expansionary monetary policy cycle that compresses interest rates and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in labor costs signals an inflationary fiscal policy cycle that compresses discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the demand for goods signals an expansionary fiscal policy cycle that compresses interest rates and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the demand for labor signals a contractionary monetary policy cycle that compresses discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
